title: Template variable output without |escape modifier
description: Disabled: In CMSMS, all Smarty variables are assigned server-side via $smarty->assign(). The |escape modifier is an HTML encoder (converts & " ' < > to entities) and is only useful when the variable contains user-submitted free-text. Static pattern matching cannot distinguish between {$user_comment} (needs escaping) and {$api_key} (does not). XSS prevention belongs in the PHP layer where the data origin is known, not blanket-applied in templates.
explanation: Variables rendered inside HTML attribute values without |escape can break out of the attribute and inject event handlers. However, in CMSMS all template variables are server-assigned and typically already sanitized or contain controlled-format data (keys, IDs, etc.). This rule produces excessive false positives without data-flow analysis.
